title: Fix mining inventory prediction — use BlockToItemDrop mapping in WorldModel.PredictMine
description:
From deep_code_audit finding 2 (88% confidence) and sprint35_audit finding 3 (98%): WorldModel.PredictMine adds the raw block name to predicted inventory (e.g. "diamond_ore" → +1 diamond_ore), while WorldStateProjector.ApplyBlockMined correctly uses BlockToItemDrop mapping (e.g. "diamond_ore" → +1 diamond). This means prediction and projection disagree on what drops, degrading replanning quality.

Fix: Have PredictMine reference the same BlockToItemDrop mapping that WorldStateProjector uses. Extract BlockToItemDrop to a shared location (e.g., CommonMinecraftBlocks.cs or a new DropMapping class) so both prediction and projection use the same source of truth.

Source: deep_code_audit (Finding 2), sprint35_audit (Finding 3)
